commit.message:
Print fidelity: pressure ink density + tilt print asymmetry (3G/3H)

3G  Pressure  ink density in print:
- Fiber ghosts: dashed center-line in ink color inside shallow cuts,
  simulating wood fibers that weren't fully cleared catching ink.
  Intensity inversely proportional to carve pressure.
- Ink edge pooling: thin dark outline at groove boundary on deep cuts,
  simulating ink collecting at sharp groove edges before transfer.
- Pressure-dependent edge noise: shallow cuts have rougher groove edges
  (28% width noise), deep cuts have clean edges (10%).

3H  Tilt  print asymmetry:
- Feathered edge on shallow wall: extended paper-color ribbon on the
  side opposite the gouge tilt, creating a soft transition.
  Enhanced tilt shift (1.6) pushes feathering outward.
- Rough wall fiber marks: thin dashed dark lines along the shallow
  edge, simulating fibers on the gradual wall catching ink.

All effects are print-only (forPrint flag); workspace rendering unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Minor fixes for CuArray tangent rules (#1045)

* fix(cuda): correct CuArray tangent rules

- Remove Vararg constructor rules that returned an uninitialised
  tangent (P(undef, _dims)); the NTuple @zero_derivative is sufficient.
- Add _new_ frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }/rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } for the DataRef inner constructor so that
  reshape and views differentiate correctly.
- Remove redundant _new_ frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } for Adjoint/Transpose; the generic
  rule produces identical output.
- Fix all four lgetfield methods: old guard (name===2||name===:dims)
  was stale; correct guard is (name===1||name===:data). Fix rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } to
  return x.dx.data (DataRef fdata) not x.dx (whole tangent CuArray),
  and fix NoPullback arg count in the three-arg variant (3, not 4).
- Add reshape test cases to exercise the DataRef _new_ path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cuda): restore Vararg constructor primitive and add reshape rule

The Vararg constructor CuArray(undef, d1, d2, ...) is a distinct
dispatch path from the NTuple form; tests called it with is_primitive=true
so it needs its own @is_primitive entry. Tangent is now zero-initialised
with zero(y) instead of the previous P(undef, _dims) which left garbage
GPU memory as a gradient accumulator.

Add a primitive rule for Base.reshape on CuMaybeComplexArray. Without
it Mooncake traces into CUDA.jl's reshape body which calls copy(DataRef)
for reference counting; that path uses llvmcall and cannot be
differentiated. reshape returns a view (shared GPU memory), so the
tangent is a reshaped view of the input tangent and NoPullback is
correct.

All 619 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cuda): remove Vararg constructor primitive; use derived rule instead

The Vararg form CuArray(undef, d1, d2, ...) is a thin wrapper in
CUDA.jl that immediately calls the NTuple Dims{N} form, which is
already a primitive via @zero_derivative. Tracing through the wrapper
is safe and produces a correct zero-initialised tangent, so no
separate primitive is needed.

Change the 1D constructor test from is_primitive=true to
is_primitive=false to reflect this.

fix(ast-engine): panic if from_path is not implemented (#98)

* fix(ast-engine): panic if `from_path` is not implemented in `Language` trait

This modifies the default trait implementation of `from_path` in `crates/ast-engine/src/language.rs` to panic with `unimplemented!("from_path is not implemented")` rather than silently returning `None`. This enforces proper implementation by types implementing the `Language` trait and prevents silent failures.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(flow): fix clippy `collapsible-if` warning in analyzer

The previous run failed because of a `clippy::collapsible_if` warning due to the `-D warnings` setup in GitHub Actions. Replaced nested if block with an `&&` condition in `crates/flow/src/incremental/analyzer.rs` to fix this issue.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(typos): fix misspelled words in dictionaries and configs

Fixed misspelled words in `_typos.toml` to prevent CI failures.
1. Replaced "inout" with "input" in `classifications/_universal_rules.json`.
2. Appended "inout", "ba", "ede", and "Bare" to `_typos.toml` `extend-ignore-identifiers-re` config since they were used as valid references/names in other locations.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(services): remove experimental `trait_alias` usage causing build failure on stable toolchains

The usage of the nightly `#![feature(trait_alias)]` caused build failures on standard stable release channels used across standard checks. Replaced the `Doc` trait alias with a regular trait definition `pub trait Doc: Clone + 'static {}` and a blanket implementation to remain compliant with stable constraints.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(ast-engine): add regression tests for `Language::from_path` default panic behavior

Added unit tests to `crates/ast-engine/src/language.rs` to confirm the panic message format correctly identifies the missing implementation type name and verify an explicit override safely succeeds.

commit.message:
fix: Restrict ACSS3 size variable conversion to width/height only

The ACSS3→ACSS4 size variable conversion regex was too broad and was
converting custom variables like --gap-m, --content-gap-m, etc. to
--gap-50, --content-gap-50, which is incorrect and breaks custom CSS.

ACSS3 only has size variants (xs, s, m, l, xl, xxl) for width and height
utilities. Other variables should not be converted.

Changed regex from:
  /var\(\s*--([a-z0-9_-]+?)-s\s*\)/
To:
  /var\(\s*--(width|height)-s\s*\)/

This preserves custom variables like --gap, --content-gap, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(perf): add PerformanceMonitor with configurable threshold alerting (PokePoke-2qj3)

Add lightweight PerformanceMonitor that checks configurable thresholds
each loop iteration and logs warnings when exceeded:

- Merge queue depth exceeding N (agents starved)
- Lock acquisition taking more than Xs (coordination breakdown)
- Loop iteration taking more than 30s (hot-loop degradation)
- Memory below critical threshold persistently (via psutil)
- Agent success rate dropping below threshold

Implementation:
- New PerformanceThresholdsConfig dataclass in config.py with clamped defaults
- Thread-safe PerformanceMonitor class with singleton pattern
- Integrated into orchestrator main loop (check_all each iteration)
- Integrated into coordination.py (lock wait alerting)

- 36 comprehensive tests covering all thresholds, thread safety, and config

fix: use stable id for variable card keys instead of WeakMap object identity (#2286)

`StepVariables` derived React keys from a `WeakMap` keyed by object
reference, but `updateVariable` in the Zustand store replaces the object
via spread (`{ ...prev, ...updates }`). Every keystroke produced a new
key → unmount/remount of the entire card subtree, breaking input focus
and degrading edit UX.

### Changes

- **`PackageVariable` type** — added `id: string` field
- **`createDefaultVariable()` / `parseManifestVariable()`** — generate
`id` via `crypto.randomUUID()`
- **`usePackageBuilderStore`** — added `backfillVariableIds()` migration
called on import and store rehydration to assign stable ids to variables
persisted without one
- **`StepVariables.tsx`** — replaced `WeakMap`-based `getVariableKey`
with `variable.id`; removed now-unused `useRef`/`useCallback` imports
- **Test** — added component test asserting DOM node identity is
preserved across edits

### Before/After

```tsx
// Before: key tied to object identity — breaks on immutable update
const variableKeyMap = useRef(new WeakMap());


// After: key tied to stable persistent id

```

commit.message:
Extract shared `useAbortableQueryRun` hook for ES|QL data hooks (#2284)

Multiple hooks (`useProfilingData`, `useProfilingAdvancedData`,
`usePanelData`, `useRankedDimensionValues`, `useEsqlQuery`) re-implement
the same "latest request wins" AbortController lifecycle. This extracts
the shared primitive and migrates the profiling hooks as a first step.

### New: `useAbortableQueryRun`

Encapsulates abort-previous → new controller → guarded callbacks:

```ts
const { run, cancel } = useAbortableQueryRun();

await run(
  (signal) => fetchSomething(signal),
  {
    onStart:   () => { setLoading(true); setError(null); },
    onSuccess: (result) => { setData(result); },
    onError:   (err) => { setError(String(err)); },
    onSettled: () => { setLoading(false); },
  },
);
```

All callbacks are guarded — they only fire if the request hasn't been
superseded by a newer `run` or explicit `cancel`.

### Migrated hooks

- **`useProfilingData`** — replaced local `abortRef` + try/catch/finally
with `run`/`cancel`. Public API unchanged.
- **`useProfilingAdvancedData`** — same migration. Public API unchanged.

### Tests

8 unit tests covering success, error, cancel, supersede, late-reject
suppression, and no-callback usage.

### Not in scope (future increments)

- `usePanelData`, `useRankedDimensionValues` — straightforward
migrations
- `useEsqlQuery` — has request-id semantics that may need the primitive
extended or kept as a specialized wrapper

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2277

Babylon.js](https://forum.babylonjs.com/t/different-behaviour-for-cpu-and-gpu-particles-after-stop-call/62654/5)

The behavior to update the GPU particles is behind a constructor option:
emitRateControl. This is false by default to not break backwards
compatibility.
